items.title: OpenAI Models, Codex, and Managed Agents Now Available on AWS
items.summary: OpenAI has announced that its models, Codex, and managed agents are now available on Amazon Web Services, significantly expanding their reach and integration capabilities for developers and enterprises.

items.title: Amazon Offers New OpenAI Products on AWS, Deepening AI Integration
items.summary: Amazon is reportedly already offering new OpenAI products through its AWS platform, indicating a rapid integration and competitive move in the cloud AI market.

items.title: The Next Phase of the Microsoft OpenAI Partnership Unveiled
items.summary: OpenAI has detailed the next phase of its strategic partnership with Microsoft, signaling continued deep collaboration on AI development and deployment.

items.title: World-R1: Reinforcing 3D Constraints for Text-to-Video Generation
items.summary: A new research paper introduces World-R1, a method that reinforces 3D constraints to improve the realism and consistency of text-to-video generation.

items.title: From Skills to Talent: Organizing Heterogeneous Agents as a Real-World Company
items.summary: Researchers propose a novel framework for organizing diverse AI agents to function cohesively as a real-world company, pushing the boundaries of multi-agent systems.

items.title: Musk Relitigates Old Friendship at OpenAI Trial
items.summary: Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI reportedly delves into the history of his friendship with the company's founders, highlighting the personal stakes in the ongoing legal battle.

items.title: Amazon Launches AI-Powered Audio Q&A Experience on Product Pages
items.summary: Amazon has introduced an AI-powered audio Q&A feature directly on its product pages, aiming to enhance customer engagement and information access.

items.title: Listen Labs Raises $69M After Viral Billboard Stunt to Scale AI Customer Interviews
items.summary: Listen Labs secured $69 million in funding following a successful viral marketing campaign, which will be used to expand its AI-driven customer interview platform.

items.title: ReVSI: Rebuilding Visual Spatial Intelligence Evaluation for Accurate Assessment of VLM 3D Reasoning
items.summary: A new evaluation framework, ReVSI, is proposed to more accurately assess the 3D reasoning capabilities and visual spatial intelligence of Vision-Language Models.

items.title: Tuna-2: Pixel Embeddings Beat Vision Encoders for Multimodal Understanding and Generation
items.summary: Tuna-2 demonstrates that pixel embeddings can outperform traditional vision encoders in multimodal understanding and generation tasks, suggesting a new direction for model architectures.

items.title: Vision-Language-Action Safety: Threats, Challenges, Evaluations, and Mechanisms
items.summary: This paper provides a comprehensive overview of the critical safety aspects in Vision-Language-Action models, covering potential threats, current challenges, evaluation methods, and mitigation mechanisms.

items.title: ClawMark: A Living-World Benchmark for Multi-Turn, Multi-Day, Multimodal Coworker Agents
items.summary: ClawMark introduces a new benchmark designed to evaluate multi-turn, multi-day, and multimodal AI coworker agents in realistic, living-world scenarios.

items.title: I Almost Missed the Most Important Announcement at Google Cloud NEXT 26
items.summary: A developer highlights a crucial announcement from Google Cloud NEXT 26 that could have significant implications for AI development and cloud services.

items.title: How I Used Gemini CLI to Orchestrate a Complex RAG Migration
items.summary: A practical guide demonstrates how Google's Gemini CLI can be effectively used to manage and orchestrate intricate Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system migrations.

items.title: On the Limits of Self-Improving in Large Language Models: The Singularity Is Not Near Without Symbolic Model Synthesis
items.summary: This paper argues that true self-improvement leading to an AI singularity in LLMs is unlikely without the integration of symbolic model synthesis capabilities.
